bead.title: agent: enforce the tracker-lock hold cap with a force-release watchdog (InstrumentCapped)
bead.description:
PROBLEM: A 4m37s land_post_land_finalize lock hold (9.2x the 30s TP-021 tracker cap) occurred with NO watchdog firing — the cap is recorded but never enforced, so a runaway hold blocks the sibling worker indefinitely.

ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:287 instruments the lock with lockmetrics.Instrument("tracker.lock", op, fn) (METRICS ONLY). The cap-enforcing lockmetrics.InstrumentCapped (cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap.go:93) with DefaultTrackerLockCap=30s (lockcap.go:23) is never wired into withTrackerLockPolicy (tracker_lock.go:215). So the cap is observed but not enforced — no force-release watchdog.

PROPOSED FIX: Wire withTrackerLockPolicy through lockmetrics.InstrumentCapped with CapConfig{Cap: lockmetrics.CapFor("tracker.lock")} so a hold past the cap fires the watchdog (stack capture, force-release, lock-violation.json evidence). Keep existing Instrument metrics behavior. This is a safety backstop; the land beads (L1/L2) remove the legitimate slow holds so the watchdog should not fire in normal operation.

NON-SCOPE: removing the slow ops (L1/L2 do that); changing the cap value.
bead.acceptance:
1. New TestTrackerLock_HoldPastCapForceReleasesAndRecordsViolation (tracker_lock_test.go): hold the lock past an overridden small cap (DDX_LOCK_CAP_TRACKER_MS); assert force-release fires and a lock-violation is recorded.
2. Existing cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap_test.go and tracker_lock_test.go (incl TestTrackerLock_SectionField) pass.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/lockmetrics/... -run 'TestTrackerLock_HoldPastCapForceReleasesAndRecordsViolation|TestTrackerLock_SectionField|TestCap' -count=1 green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
